title: betula platyphylla var. pluricostata
intro: Betula platyphylla var. pluricostata, commonly known as Korean white birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The bark of the tree is rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it useful for treating fever, pain, and inflammation, and providing health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and processed into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
